remember: Students will be able to identify alternative centres of power in a political system
Students will be able to recall the different types of alternative centres of power
Students will be able to list the characteristics of alternative centres of power
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of alternative centres of power
Students will be able to describe the role of alternative centres of power in a political system
Students will be able to understand the relationship between the state and alternative centres of power
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of alternative centres of power to real-life scenarios
Students will be able to analyze the impact of alternative centres of power on the state and society
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of alternative centres of power in achieving their goals
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the different types of alternative centres of power and their characteristics
Students will be able to compare and contrast the role of different alternative centres of power
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of alternative centres of power on the state and society

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: Students will be able to understand the concept of alternative centres of power and their role in the political system.
Students will be able to explain the different types of alternative centres of power and their characteristics.
board_exam_objectives: To test the understanding of the concept of alternative centres of power and their role in the political system.
To assess the ability to apply the concept of alternative centres of power to real-life scenarios.
To evaluate the impact of alternative centres of power on the state and society.
